Please send your data residency requirements, expected user count, and whether all shared inboxes are for EU-based customers to security@collabmail.com. Our security and enterprise teams can provide the current options, subprocessors, and contractual documentation.

If your review includes a vendor questionnaire or DPA, attach it and we'll route it to the right team.

incoming_email:
Subject: Trial limits

Hi,

What can we test during the free trial? We want to try assignment rules, templates, Slack alerts, and analytics with our real support@ inbox.
expected_reply:
Hi Ava,

During the free trial, you can connect a real shared inbox like support@ and test core collaboration features including assignments, internal notes, collision detection, email templates, and the analytics dashboard.

You can also test integrations such as Slack alerts if they are included in the plan you select for the trial. We recommend inviting a small pilot group, creating a few common templates, and using real support volume for at least one week so the analytics are meaningful.

You can start at collabmail.com/start, and our team can help review your trial setup if you send us your pilot goals.

incoming_email:
Subject: Do you have mobile apps?

Hi,

Do agents need to use desktop Gmail, or can they manage assigned emails from mobile?
expected_reply:
Hi Nadia,

Agents can use CollabMail on desktop with Gmail, and mobile access is available through the CollabMail mobile apps for common workflows such as viewing assigned conversations, replying, adding notes, and receiving notifications.

For heavier triage, template management, analytics, and admin settings, desktop is still the best experience. Mobile is most useful for staying responsive while away from a desk.

If mobile workflow is critical for your team, we recommend testing it during the trial with a few real assigned conversations.

incoming_email:
Subject: Can customers see internal notes?

Hello,

This may be basic, but can customers ever see internal notes? We want to use notes for escalation details inside support@.
expected_reply:
Hi Theo,

Customers cannot see CollabMail internal notes. Notes are visible only to teammates with access to the shared inbox conversation, and they are separate from email replies.

Internal notes are a good place for escalation context, handoff details, and manager review. Agents should still be careful to type in the note composer rather than the email reply composer before posting sensitive internal context.

If you are onboarding new agents, we recommend showing them the visual difference between internal notes and customer replies during training.

incoming_email:
Subject: Comparing Pro and Business

Hi,

We are trying to choose between Pro and Business. The main thing we care about is analytics for team performance and response times. Which plan should we pick?
expected_reply:
Hi Julia,

If analytics for team performance and response times is a main priority, Business is likely the better fit. Pro includes basic shared inbox collaboration, assignments, templates, and lighter reporting, while Business includes deeper analytics for managers and larger teams.

For example, Business is better if you need response-time trends, team workload visibility, and more detailed reporting across shared inboxes. Pro can work well if you only need basic volume and collaboration features.

You can compare plans at collabmail.com/pricing, or start on Pro and upgrade later if the analytics depth is not enough.

incoming_email:
Subject: Email forwarding or direct Gmail access?

Hi,

Does CollabMail require us to forward emails somewhere, or does it work directly with our existing Gmail inboxes?
expected_reply:
Hi Ken,

CollabMail works directly with your existing Gmail inboxes after authorized Google connection. You do not need to forward support@ or sales@ emails to a separate CollabMail address.

Once connected, CollabMail adds collaboration features such as assignments, internal notes, templates, and analytics around the Gmail conversations your team already uses.

During setup, an admin connects the shared mailbox or delegated inbox, invites teammates, and configures rules. Your customers can continue emailing the same addresses they use today.

incoming_email:
Subject: Training materials

Hello,

Do you have training materials for agents? We are rolling CollabMail out to 40 customer support reps next month.
expected_reply:
Hi Monica,

Yes, we provide training materials for agents and admins, including quick-start guides, short videos, and workflow checklists for shared inboxes.

For 40 support reps, we recommend one admin setup session, one agent training session focused on assignments, notes, templates, and collision detection, and a follow-up after the first week to review analytics and workflow questions.

Send us your rollout date and the shared inboxes involved, and we can point you to the right materials and help schedule training if your plan includes it.

incoming_email:
Subject: Can we use aliases?

Hi,

Our team replies from help@ but the actual Gmail account is support@. Can CollabMail handle aliases and keep assignments together?
expected_reply:
Hi Ravi,

CollabMail can work with Gmail aliases as long as the alias is configured correctly in Google Workspace and the connected mailbox has permission to send from that address.

Assignments and internal collaboration stay tied to the conversation in the shared inbox. The key setup step is making sure help@ is recognized as a valid sending alias for support@, so replies use the customer-facing address you expect.

During setup, test a few replies from help@ and confirm the messages appear correctly in both Gmail and the CollabMail conversation timeline.

incoming_email:
Subject: Uptime SLA

Hello,

Do you offer an uptime SLA for enterprise customers? We need this for procurement before we can approve CollabMail.
expected_reply:
Hi Hannah,

Enterprise agreements can include uptime and support commitments depending on the contract terms and support tier. We can provide the current SLA language, support response targets, and status page information during procurement review.

Please send your procurement requirements to sales@collabmail.com or book time at collabmail.com/enterprise-demo. If you have a vendor questionnaire, attach it and we'll complete the relevant sections.

For technical review, we can also share details about incident communication, monitoring, and escalation paths.

incoming_email:
Subject: Can CollabMail help reduce duplicate replies?

Hi,

Our biggest problem is two teammates answering the same customer at the same time. Does CollabMail prevent that?
expected_reply:
Hi Aaron,

Yes, CollabMail is designed to reduce duplicate replies in shared inboxes. Assignments make ownership clear, and collision detection shows when another teammate is viewing or replying to the same conversation.

Teams usually pair collision detection with simple rules: every new conversation gets assigned, agents work from their assigned queue, and internal notes are used for handoffs instead of forwarding. Email templates also help keep replies consistent.

If duplicate replies are your main pain point, you should be able to validate that quickly in a trial with your real support@ inbox.
